title: Review round 3: sieve-form post-fix-wave-2
notes:
SEC7: Running out of real complaints at P0/P1/P2: YES. Confidence: 10% chance of missed P0/P1/P2 issue. Least confident flags: (1) variables_enabled re-scanned on every evaluate() call rather than stored in CompiledScript — this is a mild per-call inefficiency (O(n) script scan) that prior rounds did not file as a defect; at the scale of Sieve scripts it is negligible but a strict reviewer might call it P3. (2) The From<ParseError> heuristic (line.is_some() -> Lex, else -> Parse) is a semantic encoding that could be wrong if the form layer ever gains position tracking — it's a maintainability landmine at P3 level. Neither rises to P0/P1/P2. The code is at a quality level where a serious Rust reviewer would approve it with minor comments (P3/P4 level), not substantive engineering objections.
SEC0: CLEAN — no panics on caller-controlled input, no unsafe, no truncating casts, no off-by-one errors, comparator enum spelling and comparator-threaded paths are consistent, validate_stmt i+=2 scoping is correct, all ParseError sites use Option<usize> (no line:0 sentinel)
